reasoning: This kernel does essentially no arithmetic and performs only a tiny amount of global memory traffic per block (`x[i]` for 256 threads), so arithmetic intensity is far below the 62 FLOP/byte ridge point and it cannot be compute-bound. The access pattern writes into shared memory with a severe bank conflict (`tile[i][0]` maps all threads in a warp to the same bank because the row stride is 32), and the kernel has very little independent work to hide that delay, so performance is dominated by waiting on memory/scoreboard latency rather than saturating DRAM bandwidth.
raw_response:

CONFIDENCE: HIGH

The access pattern writes into shared memory with a severe bank conflict (`tile[i][0]` maps all threads in a warp to the same bank because the row stride is 32), and the kernel has very little independent work to hide that delay, so performance is dominated by waiting on memory/scoreboard latency rather than saturating DRAM bandwidth.
